metadata.title: D003: Implement infrastructure deployment automation E2E

summary: Infrastructure deployment automation is well-implemented: kustomize overlays build correctly, shell scripts follow project conventions (SPDX, set -euo pipefail, log(), CC-0010 references), the Chainsaw E2E test covers all 12 required health assertions, and the two-phase kustomize pattern is correctly applied. The CI e2e-infra job follows SHA-pinned action conventions. One blocking issue: the CI job never installs the chainsaw binary, so the test step will fail with 'command not found'. Three additional major issues around secret handling and download integrity need addressing.

tests_checklist.item: Test covers all 12 required assertions (4 operator Deployments, 1 StatefulSet, ClusterIssuer, MariaDB CR, Memcached CR, ClusterSecretStore, 3 ExternalSecrets)
Test uses extended assert timeout (5m) for operator startup
Test uses v1alpha2 API version matching Chainsaw v0.3+ requirement
CI e2e-infra job runs test end-to-end successfully

code_quality_checklist.item: Shell scripts follow SPDX Apache-2.0 header convention
Shell scripts include CC-0010 feature reference
log() function uses ISO 8601 UTC timestamps matching existing pattern
Functions have single responsibility (preflight, wait_for_*, init, bootstrap)
Wait functions have configurable timeouts via environment variables
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in scripts or configs
Unseal keys passed via stdin (not as command-line arguments)
BAO_TOKEN exposure limited to bootstrap phase only
Production manifests not modified (kind-specific changes in deploy/kind/ only)
CI job uses permissions: contents: read (least privilege)

architecture_checklist.item: Two-phase kustomize pattern correctly applied (base then infrastructure)
Kustomize overlays reference production manifests without modification
OpenBao bootstrap scripts invoked as-is from CC-0009
SKIP_KIND_CREATE flag enables CI mode where cluster is pre-created
Solution is as simple as possible for the requirements

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No features beyond what was explicitly requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: Preflight checks verify all required CLI tools before deployment
Preflight checks verify Docker is running
Preflight checks detect existing kind cluster
Wait functions timeout with descriptive error messages
Bootstrap scripts validated as executable before invocation

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs (env vars) have sensible defaults
Teardown script is idempotent (succeeds if no cluster exists)
install-test-deps skips tools already at correct version
Wait loops have deadlines preventing infinite hangs

security_findings.description: install-test-deps.sh downloads 4 binaries (chainsaw, flux, kind, kubectl) via curl -fsSL with no SHA256 checksum or signature verification. A compromised CDN or supply chain attack on the upstream release could inject malicious binaries that are immediately installed with 0755 permissions and subsequently used to manage cluster infrastructure and secrets.
security_findings.fix: Add SHA256 checksum verification for each download. Most upstream projects publish checksum files (e.g., kind publishes kind-<os>-<arch>.sha256sum, flux publishes flux_<ver>_checksums.txt). Download the checksum file, verify with sha256sum -c, and abort on mismatch. Alternatively, embed expected SHA256 hashes as constants alongside the version pins.

security_findings.description: BAO_TOKEN is exported at deploy-infra.sh:346 and remains in the shell environment through step 8 (ExternalSecret wait) and until script termination. After bootstrap completes, the token is no longer needed but remains exposed to any child process or environment dump.
security_findings.fix: Add 'unset BAO_TOKEN' at the end of the openbao_bootstrap() function (after line 376) to limit the token's exposure window to only the bootstrap phase.

architecture_findings.description: The wait_for_helmreleases function interpolates the release name into a jq filter string using bash escaping. While current hardcoded names are safe, using jq --arg would be more robust and idiomatic.

issues_found.title: CI e2e-infra job does not install chainsaw — test step will fail
issues_found.description: The e2e-infra job in .github/workflows/ci.yaml runs 'chainsaw test ...' at line 83 but never installs the chainsaw binary. The job installs kind (via helm/kind-action), flux (via fluxcd/flux2/action), and go (via actions/setup-go), but there is no step to install chainsaw. The test step will fail with 'chainsaw: command not found'. Neither make install-test-deps nor any chainsaw-specific action is invoked.
issues_found.fix: Add a step before the 'Run Chainsaw E2E tests' step to install chainsaw. Options: (a) add 'run: make install-test-deps' step (installs chainsaw + other tools, some redundant with earlier actions), (b) add a dedicated step: 'run: curl -fsSL https://github.com/kyverno/chainsaw/releases/download/v0.3.1/chainsaw_linux_amd64.tar.gz | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin chainsaw', or (c) use go install if a Go module path is available. Option (a) is simplest and consistent with the documented workflow.

issues_found.title: No checksum verification for downloaded binaries in install-test-deps.sh
issues_found.description: All four binary downloads use curl -fsSL with no SHA256 checksum or signature verification. These binaries are installed with 0755 permissions and used to manage cluster infrastructure and handle secrets (kubectl, kind). This is a supply chain risk.
issues_found.fix: Add SHA256 checksum verification. Either embed expected hashes as constants alongside version pins, or download and verify the upstream-published checksum files.

issues_found.title: Unnecessary delete-before-apply creates race window for init-keys Secret
issues_found.description: The openbao_init_unseal function runs 'kubectl delete secret' (line 289) followed by 'kubectl apply' (line 295). If the script is interrupted between the delete and the apply, the init output (root token + unseal keys) is lost permanently. kubectl apply alone handles both creation and update, making the delete unnecessary.
issues_found.fix: Remove the kubectl delete secret command on lines 289-291. The subsequent kubectl apply -f - will create-or-update the Secret correctly.

issues_found.title: BAO_TOKEN remains exported after bootstrap completes
issues_found.description: BAO_TOKEN is exported at line 346 for the bootstrap scripts but remains in the environment through step 8 and script termination. This unnecessarily extends the exposure window of the root token to any child processes spawned in the remaining steps.
issues_found.fix: Add 'unset BAO_TOKEN' at the end of openbao_bootstrap(), after the log message on line 376.

issues_found.title: jq filter uses bash string interpolation instead of --arg
issues_found.description: The wait_for_helmreleases function interpolates the release name into a jq filter string via bash escaping (line 75). Using jq --arg is more robust against special characters and is idiomatic jq usage.
issues_found.fix: Replace: jq -r ".items[] | select(.metadata.name == \"${release}\") | .metadata.namespace" with: jq -r --arg name "${release}" '.items[] | select(.metadata.name == $name) | .metadata.namespace'

issues_found.title: Documentation Quick Start does not mention PATH requirement for install-test-deps
issues_found.description: The Quick Start section shows 'make install-test-deps' followed by 'make deploy-infra', but does not mention that the installed tools go to $INSTALL_DIR (default ~/.local/bin) which must be in PATH. While ~/.local/bin is typically in PATH on modern Linux, it may not be on all systems or CI runners.
issues_found.fix: Add a note after the install-test-deps command: 'Ensure $INSTALL_DIR (default: ~/.local/bin) is in your PATH.'

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a step to the CI e2e-infra job that dumps diagnostic info (kubectl get all --all-namespaces, flux logs) on failure, similar to how other infra CI jobs capture debug output for troubleshooting

The memcached-operator Deployment name 'memcached-operator-controller-manager' in chainsaw-test.yaml:68 is marked as a DECISION based on kubebuilder convention — verify this against the actual Helm chart template after first successful deployment
Consider adding shellcheck CI validation for hack/*.sh scripts to catch future shell scripting issues automatically
next_steps: Fix ISSUE-01: Add chainsaw installation step to the CI e2e-infra job (blocking)
Fix ISSUE-02: Add SHA256 checksum verification to install-test-deps.sh binary downloads
Fix ISSUE-03: Remove unnecessary kubectl delete secret before kubectl apply in deploy-infra.sh:289-291
Fix ISSUE-04: Add 'unset BAO_TOKEN' at end of openbao_bootstrap() in deploy-infra.sh
